# LevelMix

> LevelMix is a web-based audio loudness normalization SaaS that automatically
> corrects inconsistent volume levels in DJ mixes, podcasts, audiobooks, and
> long-form audio content — without requiring any technical knowledge.

LevelMix eliminates volume jumps between tracks and episodes. Upload your audio,
choose a preset, and download a professionally normalized file. No LUFS knowledge
required. No audio engineering background needed.

**The problem we solve:** One track too quiet. The next one too loud. Inconsistent
audio loses listeners — in DJ sets, podcast feeds, and long-form content alike.
LevelMix fixes this automatically.

**Core use cases:**
- DJ mixes and live sets with inconsistent loudness between tracks
- Podcast episodes with uneven volume between hosts, guests, or recordings
- Audiobook chapters requiring broadcast-standard consistency
- Content agency pipelines processing high volumes of audio
- Video editors normalizing audio across clips and projects

**Processing presets:**
- DJ Mix: -5 LUFS (club-ready, high-energy)
- Streaming: -14 LUFS (Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube)
- Podcast: -16 LUFS (industry standard for spoken word)
- Broadcast: -23 LUFS (EBU R128 / radio)

**Pricing (audio-hours based, not file count):**
- Free: 2 hours/month processing time
- Premium: €9/month or €90/year — 10 hours/month, 7-day free trial
- Professional: €24/month or €240/year — 40 hours/month, 7-day free trial
- Enterprise API: Contact for B2B pricing (async processing, hybrid billing)

**Supported formats:** MP3, WAV (paid tiers)

**Technology stack:** Go (Gin), html/template + Tailwind CSS v4, FFmpeg two-pass
loudnorm pipeline, Asynq/Redis job queue, AWS S3 (presigned URLs), Turso (libSQL),
Stripe, Hetzner VPS, Docker Compose + Traefik, Cloudflare DNS/SSL.

**Open-core architecture:** Core application logic is public on GitHub
(simonlewi/levelmix). Enterprise features (auth, payments, storage) are in a
private ee/ directory excluded from the repository.

**Operated by:** Tricode Digital AB, Sweden.

## Product

- [How It Works](https://levelmix.io/how-to-use): Step-by-step explanation of
  the automatic loudness correction process
- [Pricing](https://levelmix.io/pricing): Full tier comparison, processing limits,
  and format support breakdown
- [Get Started](https://levelmix.io/start): Free-account landing page — upload,
  choose a preset, and download corrected audio

## Documentation

- [About LevelMix](https://levelmix.io/about): Background, mission, and the
  problem we solve for audio creators
- [Privacy Policy](https://levelmix.io/privacy): GDPR-compliant data
  handling, retention periods, and user rights
- [Cookie Policy](https://levelmix.io/cookies): Cookie categories and
  consent management

## For Developers

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/simonlewi/levelmix): Open-core Go
  implementation — audio processing pipeline, job queue, HTTP handlers
- [Architecture Overview](https://github.com/simonlewi/levelmix/blob/main/README.md):
  Full technical stack, build instructions, and enterprise setup guide
- [Storage Interfaces](https://github.com/simonlewi/levelmix/blob/main/pkg/storage/interfaces.go):
  AudioStorage and MetadataStorage interface definitions for ee/ implementations
